channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Nonprofit sues the federal government over plans to paint Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool blue
item.description: The Cultural Landscape Foundation, a nonprofit advocacy organization, is asking a federal judge to halt President Trump's plans to resurface the reflecting pool on the National Mall.
item.content:encoded:
The Cultural Landscape Foundation, a nonprofit advocacy organization, is asking a federal judge to halt President Trump's plans to resurface the reflecting pool on the National Mall.

(Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Why the U.S. government is pouring millions into a Montana mining company
item.description: President Trump's visit to China highlights the lock China has on critical and strategic minerals. A big federal investment in Montana mining aims to turn that tide.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's visit to China highlights the lock China has on critical and strategic minerals. A big federal investment in Montana mining aims to turn that tide.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Traxler

item.title: Why cruise ship passengers with possible hantavirus exposure went to Nebraska
item.description: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.
item.content:encoded: The University of Nebraska is home to the only federally funded quarantine unit in the U.S. and a separate biocontainment unit that can treat people exposed to infectious diseases.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Growing number of homeless people caught up in NYC crackdown on sleeping on subways
item.description: A growing number of homeless New Yorkers are finding themselves in court after police caught them taking up more than one seat on public transit or lying on the floor of a station in recent months.
item.content:encoded: A growing number of homeless New Yorkers are finding themselves in court after police caught them taking up more than one seat on public transit or lying on the floor of a station in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Max

item.title: Oilfield theft in Texas grows more organized — and more dangerous
item.description: The state of Texas is working to crack down on the growing problem of oilfield theft. Estimated losses from stolen crude across the state total a billion dollars.
item.content:encoded: The state of Texas is working to crack down on the growing problem of oilfield theft. Estimated losses from stolen crude across the state total a billion dollars.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: Trump wants to suspend the federal gas tax as prices soar amid war with Iran
item.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.
item.content:encoded:
Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Oregon's most unexpected gubernatorial candidate? A pencil with a point
item.description: Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.
item.content:encoded:
Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.

(Image credit: Eli Imadali)
item.dc:creator: Dirk VanderHart

item.title: How do you cut your food bill? NPR wants to hear your tips
item.description: With rising food costs, NPR wants to hear your tips and hacks for reducing your bill.
item.content:encoded:
With rising food costs, NPR wants to hear your tips and hacks for reducing your bill.

(Image credit: sergeyryzhov)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: It's not shameful, it's savvy: The shoppers redefining how to save money on groceries
item.description: Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.
item.content:encoded:
Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Some researchers say restored federal funding is too little, too late
item.description: The Trump administration restored some of the billions of dollars previously frozen or withheld from research institutions and agencies. Some researchers say it's too late to save their work.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration restored some of the billions of dollars previously frozen or withheld from research institutions and agencies. Some researchers say it's too late to save their work.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle
